patch.value:
GitHub: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/548

This is backlog tracking only in the current immutable-history/read implementation lane. It belongs to the existing schema-evolution program rather than a new migration subsystem.
GH-548: rename and migrate workspace id_prefix without manual history-breaking rewrites

Add a governed SDK and CLI migration for changing a workspace id_prefix while preserving item filenames, parent/dependency references, history integrity, cursors, indexes, and external mapping evidence.

SDK exposes typed plan/apply/verify primitives and CLI is a thin adapter; dry-run lists every id and reference rewrite with collision detection; apply is locked, resumable, crash-safe, and fully audited; history verification and every parent, blocker, dependency, plan link, cursor, and derived index remain consistent; packed temp-workspace tests cover collisions, partial failure, resume, custom prefixes, closed history, and rollback guidance

Initialize and populate a workspace, change the desired id_prefix, and attempt to migrate existing ids using supported commands; no governed operation exists, leaving manual file and reference rewrites as the only path.

A dry-run-first SDK and CLI migration plans and applies an atomic, resumable id-prefix rewrite across items, graph references, history, indexes, runtime cursors, linked evidence, and import/export mappings with audited rollback-by-new-event semantics.
Changing id_prefix for an established workspace requires error-prone direct tracker file edits that can break history hashes and leave stale references.
workspace identity schema and migration engine
Long-lived or federated projects cannot safely rename their identity namespace, blocking reorganization, imports, mergers, and VCS-like workflows.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-14: all-status searches for GH-548, id_prefix rename, workspace prefix migration, and prefix reference rewrite found no canonical item. GitHub issue #548 is canonical external evidence; this item is deliberately left open and unclaimed.
